hooks_predicted: In this chapter of My China Diaries, come explore the beautiful Jiuzhaigou National Park with me.
It used to take about 8-10 hours from Chengdu by bus but with the opening of the railway station the whole journey has been cut down to around 4 hours in total.
Jiuzhaigou has been recognised as a World Heritage Site by UNESCO since 1992.
In local folklore it's said that there's a monster in the lake and there's an old man with one arm who guards the lake.
Pearl Beach was featured in the 1986 version of Xi You Ji which is also known as Journey to the West
In many ways I do think Jiuzhaigou brings together the worst and the best parts of travelling in China.
selling_points_predicted: UNESCO World Heritage Site with international recognition since 1992
Journey time dramatically reduced from 8-10 hours to just 4 hours total with new high-speed railway
Famous for spectacular waterfalls and colorful lakes, especially stunning during autumn
Convenient online ticket purchasing for foreign travelers through trip.com using passport
Entire scenic area connected by 70km of well-maintained paved boardwalks
Affordable refreshments inside the park with grilled sausages at 10 RMB (around 2-3 AUD)
Home to the highest lake in the area at an impressive altitude of 3,150 metres
Multiple valleys and routes allow for varied two-day exploration experience
Featured filming location from the iconic 1986 Journey to the West TV series
Showcase of China's exceptional natural beauty beyond major cities
Well-resourced scenic area with extensive investment in infrastructure and maintenance
Pleasant escape from intense summer heat of Chengdu and Chongqing
